description: Determine the profit-maximising output, breakeven quantity, and shutdown point of a firm using marginal revenue equals marginal cost and short-run average cost analysis

fields.name: marginal_cost_schedule
fields.label: Array of marginal cost observations at various quantities

profit_maximization.rule: Produce where MR = MC (marginal revenue equals marginal cost)
profit_maximization.perfect_competition: MR = P, so produce where P = MC
profit_maximization.concavity: MC must be rising through MR for a maximum, not a minimum

breakeven.definition: Output level at which total revenue = total cost (economic profit = 0)
breakeven.normal_profit: Accounting profit covers opportunity cost of capital

shutdown_decision.short_run_rule: Shut down if P < AVC; continue if P >= AVC (cover variable cost)
shutdown_decision.long_run_rule: Exit if P < ATC (cannot cover total cost long term)
shutdown_decision.intuition: In short run, fixed costs are sunk; operate if you cover variable cost and contribute to fixed

economies_of_scale.economies: LRAC falls as output rises — scale benefits
economies_of_scale.diseconomies: LRAC rises as output rises — coordination costs dominate

applications.corporate_capacity: Decide plant closure when price dips below AVC
applications.commodity_producers: Oil/gas shutdown decisions tied to spot vs variable cost
applications.equity_valuation: Understand margin sustainability across the cycle

compute_optimal_output.description: Determine profit-maximising quantity where P = MC

shutdown_recommended.description: Price below AVC in short run

exit_recommended.description: Price below ATC in long run

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use accounting profit as the criterion for continuing operations
anti_patterns.why: Economic profit includes opportunity cost of capital; positive accounting profit with negative economic profit means better uses of capital exist

anti_patterns.rule: Do not shut down immediately when price dips below ATC
anti_patterns.why: Short-run operation covers variable costs and contributes to fixed; shutting down means losing that contribution with fixed costs still running

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore sunk costs as irrelevant but treat fixed costs as sunk forever
anti_patterns.why: Fixed costs are sunk in the short run but avoidable in the long run via exit; apply short- vs long-run rules accordingly

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume MC curve is monotonic
anti_patterns.why: MC typically U-shaped due to diminishing returns; the intersection with MR must be on the rising portion for a maximum

decision_rules_summary.action: Short run: produce; long run: exit
